---
title: "Malaria Risk Factor Analysis"
author: "Ziporah Sandimu"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
pacman::p_load(dplyr,ggplot2, here, purrr, janitor, gtsummary, flextable,stringr,tidyr, lubridate, psych, forcats, epiR, labelled, lubridate, plotly, scales, hms, mice, e1071, caret, patchwork,stringr, rcompanion, car)
```


```{r}
set.seed(123)
n <- 1000

# ---- base variables ----
age <- sample(0:59, n, replace = TRUE)
sex <- sample(c("Male", "Female", "FEMALE", "male ", NA), n, replace = TRUE)

fever <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.4)
headache <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.5)
vomiting <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.2)
fatigue <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.4)

bednet <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.6)
stagnant_water <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.3)
sprayed_house <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.5)

wealth <- sample(c("Poor", "Middle", "Rich", "rich ", "MID", NA),
                 n, replace = TRUE)

residence <- sample(c("Urban", "Rural", "urban ", "RURAL", NA),
                    n, replace = TRUE)

temperature <- rnorm(n, mean = 37.2, sd = 1.1)
distance_health_facility <- rnorm(n, mean = 5, sd = 2)

pregnant <- ifelse(sex == "Female", rbinom(n, 1, 0.1), 0)
under5 <- ifelse(age < 5, 1, 0)

recent_travel <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.25)
previous_malaria <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.3)

# ---- introduce missingness ----
age[sample(1:n, 50)] <- NA
temperature[sample(1:n, 40)] <- NA
distance_health_facility[sample(1:n, 60)] <- NA

# ---- inject outliers ----
temperature[sample(1:n, 10)] <- c(42, 45, 50, 60, 100, rep(41, 4))
age[sample(1:n, 5)] <- c(120, 200, -5, 999, 88)

# ---- noisy sex categories (force inconsistency) ----
sex[sample(1:n, 20)] <- c("F", "M", "female", "MALE", "unknown")

# ---- logistic model (same logic but messy inputs allowed) ----
logit_p <- -3 +
  0.04 * ifelse(is.na(age), median(age, na.rm = TRUE), age) +
  1.1 * fever +
  0.8 * headache +
  0.6 * vomiting +
  0.5 * fatigue +
  -1.2 * bednet +
  0.9 * stagnant_water +
  -0.7 * sprayed_house +
  0.15 * ifelse(is.na(distance_health_facility), 5, distance_health_facility) +
  1.0 * pregnant +
  0.7 * under5 +
  0.6 * recent_travel +
  0.5 * previous_malaria +
  ifelse(wealth %in% c("Poor","poor","POOR"), 0.9,
         ifelse(wealth %in% c("Middle","MID","middle"), 0.4, 0)) +
  ifelse(residence %in% c("Rural","RURAL","rural"), 0.8, 0)

prob_malaria <- 1 / (1 + exp(-logit_p))
malaria <- rbinom(n, 1, prob_malaria)

# ---- FINAL messy dataset ----
malaria_data_dirty <- data.frame(
  age,
  sex,
  fever = fever,                  # intentionally NOT factor
  headache = headache,
  vomiting = vomiting,
  fatigue = fatigue,
  bednet = bednet,
  stagnant_water = stagnant_water,
  sprayed_house = sprayed_house,
  wealth,
  residence,
  temperature,
  distance_health_facility,
  pregnant,
  under5,
  recent_travel,
  previous_malaria,
  malaria = malaria
)

# ---- introduce duplicates ----
malaria_data_dirty <- rbind(
  malaria_data_dirty,
  malaria_data_dirty[sample(1:n, 20), ]
)

head(malaria_data_dirty)

```

```{r}
str(malaria_data_dirty)
summary(malaria_data_dirty)
colSums(is.na(malaria_data_dirty))
```

```{r}
malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_dirty %>% 
 mutate(
    sex = str_trim(str_to_title(sex)),
    wealth = str_trim(str_to_title(wealth)),
    residence = str_trim(str_to_title(residence))
 )
```

```{r}
malaria_data_clean %>%
  distinct(malaria)

```

```{r}
malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean %>%
  mutate(
    sex = case_when(
      sex %in% c("Female", "F") ~ "Female",
      sex %in% c("Male", "M") ~ "Male",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    wealth = case_when(
      wealth %in% c("Middle", "Mid") ~ "Middle",
      wealth == "Rich" ~ "Rich",
      wealth == "Poor" ~ "Poor",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
)
  )
```

```{r}
malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean %>%
  distinct()
```

```{r}
ggplot(malaria_data_clean, aes(x = age)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 5, fill = "skyblue") +
    labs(title = "Age Distribution")
```

```{r}
 # replacing missing age values
  malaria_data_clean %>%
  filter(age < 0 | age >60)

malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean  %>%
  mutate(age = if_else(age < 0 | age > 60, NA, age)) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(age))

skewness(malaria_data_clean$age, na.rm = TRUE)
```


```{r}
malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean  %>%
  mutate(
    age = coalesce(age, round(mean(age, na.rm = TRUE)), 0)
  )
```


```{r}

theme_who <- function() {
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      # Titles
      plot.title = element_text(
        hjust = 0.5,
        size = 16,
        face = "bold"
      ),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(
        hjust = 0.5,
        size = 14,
        face = "bold"
      ),
      
      # Axis titles
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      
      # Grid (clean look)
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey85"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      
      # Background
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
      plot.background = element_rect(color ="black",  fill = "white"),
      
      # Legend
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
}


ggplot(malaria_data_clean, aes(x = age)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)),#creates density, scaled version of count)
                 bins = 13, 
                 alpha = 0.6, # controls transparency of the bars
                 fill = "skyblue", 
                 color = "black") +
  
  #creating a smooth curve to show the distribution of the data, 
   geom_density(
               color = "darkred",
               linewidth = 1.2
               ) +
  #Recomputes histogram bins, Finds count in each bin, Places text at top of bars, Displays the count values
  stat_bin(
    bins = 13,
    aes(y = after_stat(density), label = after_stat(count)),
    geom = "text",
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 3
  ) +
 
  #controls the x axis labels
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 60, 5),
    limits = c(0, 60)
  ) +
    labs(title = "Age Distribution",
         subtitle = "Age")+
  
  theme_who()
```

```{r}
ggplot(malaria_data_clean, aes(x = temperature)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "orange", colour = "black") +
  labs(title = "Temperature Distribution")+
  theme_who()
```

```{r}
 #replacing outlier temperature values to NA
malaria_data_clean %>%
 filter(temperature < 30|temperature > 42)

malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean  %>%
  mutate(
    temperature = if_else(temperature < 30|temperature > 42, NA, temperature)
  )
skewness(malaria_data_clean$temperature, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean  %>%
  mutate(
    temperature = coalesce(temperature, mean(temperature, na.rm = TRUE))
  )
```

```{r}
#univariate analysis

ggplot(malaria_data_clean, aes(x = distance_health_facility)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "green", colour = "black") 
  labs(title = "Distance of the health facility")+
  theme_who()

```

```{r}

```

```{r}
 # replacing missing sex values
malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean  %>%
  mutate(
    sex= if_else(is.na(sex), "Unknown", sex)
  )
```

```{r}
  #replacing missing residence values
mode_residence <- malaria_data_clean %>% 
  count(residence) %>% 
  arrange(desc(n)) %>% 
  slice(1) %>% 
  pull(residence)

malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean  %>%
  mutate(residence = if_else(is.na(residence), mode_residence, residence))
```

```{r}
#replacing missing wealth values
mode_wealth <- malaria_data_clean %>%
  count(wealth) %>% 
  arrange(desc(wealth)) %>% 
  slice(1) %>% 
  pull(wealth)

malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean  %>%
  mutate(wealth = if_else(is.na(wealth), mode_wealth, wealth))
```

```{r}
#replacing missing distance values
median_value <- median(malaria_data_clean$distance_health_facility, na.rm = TRUE)

malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean %>%
  mutate(distance_health_facility = if_else(
    is.na(distance_health_facility),
    median_value,
    distance_health_facility
  ))
```

```{r}
#replacing missing in pregnacy
malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean %>%
  mutate(
    pregnant = as.character(pregnant),
    pregnant = if_else(is.na(pregnant), "Unknown", pregnant)
    )
```

```{r} 
#replacing NA malaria values
# malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean %>%
#   mutate(
#     malaria = if_else(is.na(malaria), NA, malaria)
#   )
```

```{r}

```

```{r}
malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean %>%
  mutate(
    pregnant = case_when(
      pregnant == "1" ~ "Yes",
      pregnant == "0" ~ "No",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    ),
    pregnant = as.factor(pregnant)
  )
```

```{r}
#handling malaria missing values
malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(malaria))

malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean %>%
  mutate(malaria = factor(malaria,
                          levels = c(0, 1),
                          labels = c("Negative", "Positive")))
```

```{r}
malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean %>%
  mutate(
    sex = as.factor(sex),
    wealth = as.factor(wealth),
    residence = as.factor(residence)
  )
str(malaria_data_clean)
```

```{r}
binary_vars <- c("fever", "headache", "vomiting", "fatigue",
                 "bednet", "stagnant_water", "sprayed_house",
                 "recent_travel", "previous_malaria", "under5")

malaria_data_clean <- malaria_data_clean %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(binary_vars), ~ factor(
    ifelse(.x == 1, "Yes",
           ifelse(.x == 0, "No", NA)),
    levels = c("No", "Yes")
  )))

str(malaria_data_clean)
```

```{r}
table(malaria_data_clean$malaria)
prop.table(table(malaria_data_clean$malaria))
```

```{r}
#It creates boxplots to compare age, temperature, and distance to a health facility between malaria-positive and malaria-negative individuals.

long_data <- malaria_data_clean %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(age, temperature, distance_health_facility),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value"
  )
ggplot(long_data, aes(x = malaria, y = value, fill = malaria)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Numeric variables by Malaria status",
    x = "Malaria status",
    y = "Value"
  ) +
  theme_who()
```


```{r}
#categorical variables analysis
table(malaria_data_clean$sex, malaria_data_clean$malaria)

table(malaria_data_clean$wealth, malaria_data_clean$malaria)

table(malaria_data_clean$residence, malaria_data_clean$malaria)
```


```{r}
table(malaria_data_clean$fever, malaria_data_clean$malaria)
prop.table(table(malaria_data_clean$fever, malaria_data_clean$malaria), 1)

table(malaria_data_clean$headache, malaria_data_clean$malaria)
prop.table(table(malaria_data_clean$headache, malaria_data_clean$malaria), 1)

table(malaria_data_clean$fatigue, malaria_data_clean$malaria)
prop.table(table(malaria_data_clean$fatigue, malaria_data_clean$malaria), 1)


table(malaria_data_clean$stagnant_water, malaria_data_clean$malaria)
prop.table(table(malaria_data_clean$stagnant_water, malaria_data_clean$malaria), 1)

```



```{r}
#visual comparison between symptoms and malaria status
pivot_longer_data <- malaria_data_clean %>%
  select(fever, headache, fatigue, stagnant_water, bednet, sprayed_house, malaria) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -malaria, names_to = "symptom", values_to = "value") %>%
  
  mutate(symptom = factor(symptom,
                        levels = c("fever", "headache", "fatigue",
                                   "stagnant_water", "bednet", "sprayed_house")))
  
  ggplot(pivot_longer_data, aes(x = value, fill = malaria)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  facet_wrap(~ symptom) +
  labs(title = "Symptoms vs Malaria",
       y = "Proportion",
       x = "Symptom Presence") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent)+
  theme_set(theme_who())
```

```{r}
#creating a table doing a chi-square test, calculating the strength using cramer's V and storing them in a table 
symptoms <- c("fever", "headache", "fatigue", 
              "stagnant_water", "bednet", "sprayed_house")

results <- lapply(symptoms, function(var) {
  
  tab <- table(malaria_data_clean[[var]],
               malaria_data_clean$malaria,
               useNA = "no")
  
  test <- chisq.test(tab)
  
  
  data.frame(
    symptom = var,
    p_value = test$p.value,
    significant = ifelse(test$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    cramers_v = cramerV(tab)
  )
})

results_df <- do.call(rbind, results)

results_df <- results_df %>%
  mutate(
    strength = case_when(
      cramers_v < 0.1 ~ "Very weak",
      cramers_v < 0.3 ~ "Weak",
      cramers_v < 0.5 ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ "Strong"
    )
  )
results_df

# results <- lapply(symptoms, function(var) {
#   tab <- table(malaria_data_clean[[var]], malaria_data_clean$malaria)
#   test <- chisq.test(tab)
#   
#   data.frame(
#     symptom = var,
#     p_value = test$p.value,
#     cramers_v = cramerV(tab)
#   )
# })

```

```{r}
#checks whether age, temperature, and distance to a health facility differ between malaria-positive and malaria-negative individuals using the Wilcoxon test.

numeric_vars <- c("age", "temperature", "distance_health_facility")

lapply(numeric_vars, function(var) {
  
  formula <- as.formula(paste(var, "~ malaria"))
  
  test <- wilcox.test(formula, data = malaria_data_clean)
  
  data.frame(
    variable = var,
    p_value = test$p.value,
    significant = ifelse(test$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
  )
}) %>% bind_rows()
```

```{r}

```

```{r}
#checking multicollinearityy for symptoms
symptom_model <- glm(malaria ~ fever + headache + fatigue + vomiting,
                     data = malaria_data_clean,
                     family = binomial)

vif(symptom_model)
```

```{r}
#checking multicollinearityy for FEVER AND TEMPERATURE
fever_temp_model <- glm(malaria ~ fever + temperature,
                        data = malaria_data_clean,
                        family = binomial)

vif(fever_temp_model)
```

```{r}
#CHECKING FOR MULTICOLLINEARITY for environmental factors
env_model <- glm(malaria ~ bednet + sprayed_house + stagnant_water,
                 data = malaria_data_clean,
                 family = binomial)

vif(env_model)
```
```{r}
#checking for multicollinearity for age and under 5
demo_model <- glm(malaria ~ age + under5,
                  data = malaria_data_clean,
                  family = binomial)

vif(demo_model)
```

```{r}
#logistic regression model
final_model <- glm(malaria ~ age + temperature +
                     headache + fatigue + stagnant_water + bednet + sprayed_house,
                   data = malaria_data_clean,
                   family = binomial)

summary(final_model)

exp(coef(final_model))

exp(confint(final_model))

vif(final_model)
```

```{r}
#evaluating prediction ability of the model
pred_probs <- predict(final_model, type = "response")

pred_class <- factor(ifelse(predict(final_model, type = "response") > 0.5, 1, 0),
                     levels = c(0, 1),
                     labels = c("Negative", "Positive"))

table(Predicted = pred_class, Actual = malaria_data_clean$malaria)
```
